rulings.Case Title: DORAN MCGEE ET AL VS. BROWN FAMILY TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of 5th And 6th Causes Of Action Of First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS PRIVATE INVESTMENT EQUITY, LLC AND KEVIN DAWSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party failed its burden to show that it is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law, specifically no authority is cited for the proposition that roommates sharing an apartment are not "tenants" within the meaning of the SFRO. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGEMENT GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend to allege facts (not conclusions) to support each element of each cause of action. Plaintiff shall also allege specific facts and specific dates regarding lack of legal capacity. All allegations must be in clear and concise language. Plaintiff may, but it not required to, use a form complaint. Plaintiff should refrain form extensively quoting law and legal treatises in the complaint. Plaintiff shall have 60 days to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 1030 POLK STREET ASSOCIATES VS. 2428 CLEMENT STREET RETAIL INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT 2428 CLEMENT STREET RETAIL INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for unlawful detainer based on non-payment of rent is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Demurrer for the cause of action based on 3-Day Notice to Cure or Quit is OVERRULED. The notice sufficiently states the basis for Plaintiff's belief that Defendant is in breach of the lease. Whether the breach in fact occurred, is an anticipatory breach (as discussed in opposition) or is not a breach at all is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
